quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Plasma membrane"?

options.text: Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes during prophase I of meiosis; increases genetic variation
The percentage of offspring showing recombinant phenotypes; used to calculate the distance between linked genes
A phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins that controls what enters and exits the cell; selectively permeable
RNA polymerase reads the DNA template strand 3'→5' and synthesizes complementary mRNA 5'→3'; occurs in the nucleus in eukaryotes

quiz_questions.explanation: "Plasma membrane" is defined as: A phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins that controls what enters and exits the cell; selectively permeable. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Nucleus"?

options.text: Membrane-bound organelle containing chromatin (DNA + histones); site of DNA replication, transcription, and ribosome assembly (nucleolus)
A group consisting of a common ancestor and ALL of its descendants; the fundamental unit in modern classification
Genes on the X chromosome; males (XY) express all X-linked alleles; conditions like hemophilia and color blindness are more common in males
The number of different species in an area; generally increases with area, decreases with latitude, and increases toward the tropics

quiz_questions.explanation: "Nucleus" is defined as: Membrane-bound organelle containing chromatin (DNA + histones); site of DNA replication, transcription, and ribosome assembly (nucleolus). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Endoplasmic reticulum"?

options.text: Rough ER (with ribosomes): protein synthesis and modification; Smooth ER: lipid synthesis, detoxification, calcium storage
The number of individuals contributing genetically to the next generation; usually much smaller than census size due to unequal sex ratios, variance in reproductive success
No mutation, no migration, random mating, no selection, large population size; violations drive evolution
A gene encoding a protein that inhibits cell division (e.g., p53 triggers apoptosis in damaged cells, Rb blocks G1/S transition)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Endoplasmic reticulum" is defined as: Rough ER (with ribosomes): protein synthesis and modification; Smooth ER: lipid synthesis, detoxification, calcium storage. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Mitochondria"?

options.text: A DNA sequence upstream of a gene (e.g., TATA box) where RNA polymerase and transcription factors bind to initiate transcription
The first photosystem in the light reactions; absorbs 680nm light, splits water, and passes excited electrons to the electron transport chain
Double-membrane organelle where cellular respiration produces ATP; has own circular DNA, supporting the endosymbiotic theory
A signaling molecule (hormone, neurotransmitter, growth factor) that binds specifically to a receptor protein

quiz_questions.explanation: "Mitochondria" is defined as: Double-membrane organelle where cellular respiration produces ATP; has own circular DNA, supporting the endosymbiotic theory. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Endomembrane system"?

options.text: The percentage of offspring showing recombinant phenotypes; used to calculate the distance between linked genes
A 10-step metabolic pathway in the cytoplasm that converts glucose (6C) into 2 pyruvate (3C), producing 2 net ATP and 2 NADH
Normal cells stop dividing when they contact neighboring cells; cancer cells lose this control (contact inhibition)
The network of ER, Golgi, lysosomes, vesicles, and plasma membrane connected by vesicle trafficking for protein processing and transport

quiz_questions.explanation: "Endomembrane system" is defined as: The network of ER, Golgi, lysosomes, vesicles, and plasma membrane connected by vesicle trafficking for protein processing and transport. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins that controls what enters and exits the cell; selectively permeable"?

options.text: Dominant and recessive
Photosystem II (P680)
Allele frequency
Plasma membrane

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Plasma membrane".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Membrane-bound organelle containing chromatin (DNA + histones); site of DNA replication, transcription, and ribosome assembly (nucleolus)"?

options.text: Allopatric speciation
Homologous structures
Ribosome
Nucleus

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Nucleus".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Rough ER (with ribosomes): protein synthesis and modification; Smooth ER: lipid synthesis, detoxification, calcium storage"?

options.text: Germline vs. somatic mutation
Endoplasmic reticulum
Outgroup
Hydrogen bond

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Endoplasmic reticulum".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Double-membrane organelle where cellular respiration produces ATP; has own circular DNA, supporting the endosymbiotic theory"?

options.text: Krebs cycle (citric acid cycle)
Germline vs. somatic mutation
Mitochondria
Linked genes

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Mitochondria".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The network of ER, Golgi, lysosomes, vesicles, and plasma membrane connected by vesicle trafficking for protein processing and transport"?

options.text: Ecosystem services
Meiosis
Denaturation
Endomembrane system

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Endomembrane system".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Cell Structure is accurate?

options.text: Recombination frequency between linked genes (calculated from testcross offspring) indicates the distance between genes on a chromosome, measured in centimorgans (cM) or map units.
DNA Replication and Repair describes the semiconservative process by which DNA is copied before cell division.
While most mutations are neutral or harmful, some confer advantages (e.g., sickle cell heterozygote advantage against malaria).
Cell Structure covers the organization of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cell Structure as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Cell Structure?

options.text: Eukaryotic cells compartmentalize functions in membrane-bound organelles: the nucleus (DNA storage), endoplasmic reticulum (protein/lipid synthesis), Golgi apparatus (modification and sorting), mitochondria (ATP production), and chloroplasts...
Water is split (photolysis) at PSII, releasing O₂.
The specific bond orientation (α vs. β linkages) determines digestibility and function: α-1,4 bonds in starch are digestible by humans, while β-1,4 bonds in cellulose are not.

The Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium (p² + 2pq + q² = 1, p + q = 1) serves as a null hypothesis: populations are in equilibrium only when there is no mutation, migration, selection, drift, or non-random mating.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cell Structure as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Cell Structure?

options.text: NADP⁺ is reduced to NADPH at PSI.
The endomembrane system connects these organelles through vesicle trafficking.
Alternative splicing enables one gene to produce multiple protein variants.
Monohybrid crosses (Aa × Aa → 3:1 phenotypic ratio) and dihybrid crosses (AaBb × AaBb → 9:3:3:1 ratio) predict offspring ratios, testable with chi-square analysis.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cell Structure as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Cell Structure?

options.text: Cellular Respiration: Krebs Cycle (citric acid cycle) occurs in the mitochondrial matrix when pyruvate is converted to acetyl-CoA (producing CO₂ and NADH), which then enters the cycle.
DNA polymerase's proofreading (3'→5' exonuclease) and mismatch repair maintain a remarkably low error rate (~1 error per 10⁹ bases).
Structure-function relationships are evident at every level of cellular organization.
Monohybrid crosses (Aa × Aa → 3:1 phenotypic ratio) and dihybrid crosses (AaBb × AaBb → 9:3:3:1 ratio) predict offspring ratios, testable with chi-square analysis.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cell Structure as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Cell Structure?

options.text: DNA Replication and Repair describes the semiconservative process by which DNA is copied before cell division.

The three stages—reception (ligand binding), transduction (signal relay/amplification through phosphorylation cascades), and response (gene expression, enzyme activation, or cell movement)—allow cells to respond to their environment.
Adaptive radiation (rapid diversification from a single ancestor into multiple ecological niches) often follows mass extinctions or colonization of new environments.
Cell Structure covers the organization of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Cell Structure as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Cell Structure?

options.text: Antibiotic targeting of prokaryotic-specific structures (70S ribosomes, cell wall synthesis) that differ from eukaryotic cells, enabling selective bacterial killing
Lynch syndrome (hereditary nonpolyposis colorectal cancer) caused by inherited mutations in mismatch repair genes, increasing cancer risk
Genetic engineering using the universal genetic code to express human insulin in E. coli bacteria, producing recombinant insulin for diabetes treatment
Caloric content of foods measured by the energy released during complete oxidation: carbohydrates ~4 kcal/g, lipids ~9 kcal/g, proteins ~4 kcal/g

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cell Structure.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Cell Structure?

options.text: Yellowstone-to-Yukon Conservation Initiative creating wildlife corridors spanning 3,200 km to connect fragmented habitats for grizzly bears, wolves, and elk migration
DNA fingerprinting using STR analysis for forensic identification, with Combined DNA Index System (CODIS) databases matching crime scene samples to individuals
Darwin's finch beak evolution on Daphne Major Island: drought years selected for larger beaks (to crack harder seeds), directly observed by Peter and Rosemary Grant over 40 years
Cancer cell organelle abnormalities: enlarged nuclei, increased rough ER (elevated protein synthesis), and altered mitochondrial metabolism (Warburg effect)

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cell Structure.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Cell Structure applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Endosymbiotic theory evidence: mitochondria and chloroplasts have double membranes, own circular DNA, 70S ribosomes, and replicate independently
Metabolic syndrome and diabetes: dysregulation of glucose metabolism affecting cellular respiration, insulin signaling, and energy storage pathways
Darwin's finch beak evolution on Daphne Major Island: drought years selected for larger beaks (to crack harder seeds), directly observed by Peter and Rosemary Grant over 40 years
Yellowstone-to-Yukon Conservation Initiative creating wildlife corridors spanning 3,200 km to connect fragmented habitats for grizzly bears, wolves, and elk migration

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cell Structure.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Cell Structure?

options.text: Antibiotic targeting of prokaryotic-specific structures (70S ribosomes, cell wall synthesis) that differ from eukaryotic cells, enabling selective bacterial killing
Golden Rice engineered with β-carotene biosynthesis genes to combat vitamin A deficiency in developing countries, preventing blindness in millions of children
Coral bleaching when heat stress damages photosystem proteins in symbiotic zooxanthellae, disrupting photosynthesis and causing coral death
Cancer immunotherapy using checkpoint inhibitors (anti-PD-1, anti-CTLA-4) to re-enable the immune system's ability to attack cancer cells

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cell Structure.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Cell Structure in practice?

options.text: Cancer cell organelle abnormalities: enlarged nuclei, increased rough ER (elevated protein synthesis), and altered mitochondrial metabolism (Warburg effect)
Rising atmospheric CO₂ levels increasing carbon fixation rates in some plants (CO₂ fertilization effect) but potentially decreasing nutritional quality of crops
Madagascar's unique biodiversity (90% of species found nowhere else) explained by 88 million years of isolation after separating from Africa and India
Feedback inhibition in the cholesterol synthesis pathway: statins mimic the substrate of HMG-CoA reductase, acting as competitive inhibitors to lower cholesterol levels

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Cell Structure.
